title: A system for removal of solid waste from water bodies
abstract: A system (100) for collection and removal of solid waste from a water body is disclosed. The system (100) operates using conveyors of specific materials and specially designed internal structures that aid in capturing of solid waste and its subsequent deposit on banks where it can be segregated by waste segregation plant. One unit of the main structure contains two types of conveyor belts (FCB, SCB) which together act to collect waste from river and deposit waste to banks and garbage concentrators (C). The system construction is in such a manner, that it provides a bucket like means (9), attached at regular intervals onto the first conveyor belt (FCB), and adapted to scoop out solid waste from the water body when the first conveyor belt (FCB) is functional. The bucket (9) acts as the means to contain the waste while it is being pulled out of the water body.
claims:
Claims We claim: 1. A system (100) for removal of solid waste from a water body comprising: - a first conveyor belt (FCB) adapted to be partially submerged in the water body at an angle with a surface of the water body; - one or more bucket like means (9) are attached at regular intervals onto the first conveyor belt (FCB), and adapted to scoop out solid waste from the water body when the first conveyor belt (FCB) is functional. 2. The system (100) as claimed in claim 1, wherein the first conveyor belt (FCB), or the bucket like means (9), or both are having a porous structure to at least allow water to flow through it. 3. The system (100) as claimed in any of the claims 1 or 2 comprising: - a second conveyor belt (SCB) is in a functional coupling with the first conveyor belt (FCB) to receive the waste from each of the buckets (9) when the buckets (9) are out of the water. 4. The system (100) as claimed in claim 3, wherein the first conveyor belt (FCB) is having two ends and two sides, a first end (FCB') which is adapted to be placed in water for picking up waste, and a second end (FCB") which is adapted to remain out of water when the first conveyor belt (FCB) is functional, and is placed in proximity to the second conveyor belt (SCB), so that the buckets (9) while moving from a first side of the first conveyor belt (FCB) after picking up waste from the water body to a second side of the conveyor belt transfers the waste carried by the bucket (9) onto the second conveyor belt 

 (SCB). 5. The system (100) as claimed in any of the claims 3 or 4, wherein the second conveyor belt (SCB) comprises a remote end which is remote with respect to the first conveyor belt (FCB), the system (100) further comprising: - a movable track holding a plurality of buckets (9) and adapted to move the buckets (9), wherein the track runs beneath the remote end of the conveyor belt. 6. The system (100) as claimed in claim 5 comprising: - a sensor placed on the bucket (9), or placed in proximity of the bucket (9), or placed in proximity to the remote end of the second conveyor belt (SCB), and adapted to sense a level of waste in the bucket (9), and generate a level data; - a first controller adapted to receive and process the level data, and adapted to generate a movement trigger; and - a movement mechanism adapted to receive the movement trigger, and adapted to move the movement track. 7. The system (100) as claimed in any of the claims 1 to 6, wherein the bucket like means (9) is formed by physically coupling a holding structure (1) to the first conveyor belt (FCB) at an angle of 90 degree or lesser with respect to a surface of the first conveyor belt (FCB), wherein the holding structure (1) is adapted to hold the waste while pulling out of the water body when the first conveyor belt (FCB) is in function. 8. The system (100) as claimed in claim 7, wherein the bucket like means (9) further comprises one or two side structures (2) either placed in proximity to the holding 

 structure (1) or physically coupled to the holding structure (1) such that the side structure (2) are perpendicular or substantially perpendicular to the plate, so that the side structure (2) is adapted to support in holding the waste while the waste is held by the bucket-like means (9). 9. The system (100) as claimed in any of the claim 7 or 8, wherein the holding structure (1) or the side structure (2), or both, can be having a continuous structure, or interleaving structure made of multiple plates. 10. The system (100) as claimed in any of the claims 1 to 9 comprising: - a fluid jet placed in proximity to the second end (FCB") of the first conveyor belt (FCB) which is away from the first end (FCB') submerged in water body, and is adapted to throw a pressurized fluid jet on to an inner surface of the bucket-like means (9), and the first conveyor belt (FCB). 11. The system (100) as claimed in claim 10, wherein the fluid jet is placed in proximity to the second end (FCB") of the first conveyor belt (FCB) and within a volume bounded by the first conveyor belt (FCB). 12. The system (100) as claimed in any of the claims 1 to 11 comprises: - a base (B) onto which the system (100) holds into the water body, the base (B) is made up of resilient material. 13. The system (100) as claimed in claim 12, wherein the base (B) is made up of structure with alternate layers of a high elasticity material, and a low elasticity material. 14. The system (100) as claimed in claim 13, wherein the high elasticity material can be stainless steel, structural steel, or wrought iron, or combination thereof or any other similar material/combination, and the low elasticity material can be natural rubber, neoprene, sorbothane, or combination thereof or any other similar material/combination. 15. The system (100) as claimed in any of the claims 13 or 14, wherein structure of the alternate layers of the high elasticity material, and the low elasticity material is encapsulated in a corrosion resistant covering. 16. The system (100) as claimed in any of the claims 13 to 15, wherein the base (B) further comprises support structure (4) physically coupling base (B) to the conveyor belts. 17. The system (100) as claimed in claim 16, wherein the support structure (4) is made of tubes (6) which are made of a corrosion resistant material, or are having a corrosion resistant coating. 18. The system (100) as claimed in any of the claims 1 to 17 comprising: - a waste concentrator (C) functionally coupled to the first end (FCB') of the first conveyor belt (FCB), and adapted to concentrate the waste towards the first end (FCB') of the first conveyor belt (FCB). 19. The system (100) as claimed in any of the claims 1 to 18 comprising: 

 - an impeller like structure supported on an upstream facing surface of the waste concentrator (C), wherein the impeller like structure is powered by a motor, and is used to push accumulated waste towards the First Conveyor Belt (FCBâ). 20. The system (100) as claimed in claim 19 comprising: a stiff sheet coupled with a driving chain such that there is no relative motion between the driving chain and the stiff sheet. 21. The system (100) as claimed in claim 19, wherein the stiff sheet is used to cover an area between two extensions on sides of waste concentrator, thereby pushing the accumulated waste towards the First Concentrator Belt (FCB). 22. The system as claimed in claim 20, wherein the stiff sheet is covered with a hydrophobic coating to prevent wet waste from sticking on its surface. 23. The system (100) as claimed in claim 18 comprises two side panels (3) attached to two sides of the first conveyor belt (FCB), wherein the waste concentrator (C) is formed by two mechanical structures (5) which are placed in proximity to the first end (FCB') of the first conveyor belt (FCB), or physically connected to the two side panels (3) at the first end (FCB') of the first conveyor belt (FCB) or in proximity to the first end (FCB') of the first conveyor belt (FCB), the two mechanical structures (5) are placed at an acute angle with respect to the two side panels (3) of the first conveyor belt (FCB) and extends outwards with respect to the first conveyor belt (FCB), and the two mechanical structures (5) are placed such that they are at least partially outside the surface of the water body. 24. The system (100) as claimed in claim 23, wherein the two side panels (3) are made of a corrosion resistant material, or are having a corrosion resistant coating. 25. The system (100) as claimed in claim 24, wherein the two mechanical structures (5) are porous in nature to allow at least the water to flow out through it. 26. The system (100) as claimed in any of the claims 24 or 25, wherein the two mechanical structures (5) each are made of a plurality of modules (7) physically coupled to each other, wherein each module (7) can be a planar sheet or blocks, or combination thereof. 27. The system (100) as claimed in claim 26 comprising: - a tensioning mechanism (8) connecting the modules (7) to the side panels (3). 28. The system (100) as claimed in any of the claims 26 or 27, wherein the modules (7) are made of a material to enable the modules (7) to float on water. 29. The system (100) as claimed in any of the claims 27 or 28 comprising: - one or more buoys physically coupled to the modules (7). 30. The system (100) as claimed in any of the claims 23 to 29 comprising: - a mobility mechanism adapted to move the two mechanical structures (5) either to change the angle of the two mechanical structures (5) with respect to the two side panels (3), or to move vertically with respect to the surface of the water body, or combination thereof. 31. The system (100) as claimed in any of the claims 1 to 30 comprising: - a fluid pumping mechanism (D) adapted to throw a pressurized fluid onto upstream end of the first conveyor belt (FCB). 32. The system (100) as claimed in claim 31, wherein the fluid pumping mechanism further includes a pipe which has an opening placed such that it throws the pressurized fluid onto upstream of the first conveyor belt (FCB). 33. The system (100) as claimed in claim 33, wherein the opening of the pipe has a structure which is adapted to throw fluid in a sheet like fashion. 34. The system (100) as claimed in any of the claims 31 to 32 comprising: - a blockage sensor adapted to sense a blockage into or in proximity to the movement path of the first conveyor belt (FCB), and generate a blockage related data; - a second controller adapted to receive and the blockage related data, and accordingly control the fluid pumping mechanism (D). 35. The system (100) as claimed in any of the claims 1 to 34 comprising: - a third controller adapted to control the movement of the first conveyor belt (FCB), or the second conveyor belt (SCB), or both. 36. The system (100) as claimed in claim 35 comprising: - a flow sensor placed in proximity or into the water body, and adapted to sense a flow of the water in the water body and to generate a flow related data; 

 - the third controller is adapted to receive and process the flow related data and is adapted to control the movement of the first conveyor belt (FCB), or the second conveyor belt (SCB), or both. 37. The system (100) as claimed in any of the claims 1 to 36, wherein one or more functional elements of the system (100) are powered using solar energy, wherein the functional elements are defined as parts of the system (100) which are adapted to either trigger movement or facilitate movement, or generate motion in or of any elements of the system (100). 38. The system (100) as claimed in claim 37 comprising: - a support structure (4) positioned structurally coupled to at least the second end (FCB") of the first conveyor belt (FCB) or to a part of the second conveyor belt (SCB) functionally or to both; and - one or more solar panels (10) placed onto the support structure (4), wherein the solar power is adapted to generate solar energy to power one or more functional elements of the system (100).
